commentary_text: The market assigns Ecuador a 39% win probability versus the model's 57%, a 17-percentage-point gap favoring the model's confidence in the favorite. This hedging could reflect sportsbook uncertainty around recent team form, lineup changes, or injury status that the model cannot access and that may have shifted since the last rating update. The market also elevates the draw probability to 31% compared to the model's 27%, further distributing belief across outcomes. Both sources agree Ecuador is favored, but the market's wider distribution of probability—particularly its uplift on Ivory Coast's chances to 29%—suggests real-money flows are pricing in contingencies the statistical model does not see. The dashboard surfaces this as a signal of information asymmetry, not a judgment on which view will prove correct.
